chapter_info.subject: Social Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: Universal Franchise and India's Electoral System

Core Concept: Universal Adult Franchise: Every Indian citizen aged 18 and above has the right to vote (Article 326) regardless of social, economic, or gender identity.
The principle of 'one person, one vote, one value' ensures political equality and empowers ordinary citizens to shape their nation's future.
India adopted this bold inclusive vision right from independence (1950), demonstrating deep trust in the common man compared to many Western nations.
The Machinery of Elections: ECI: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an independent constitutional body established in 1950 to oversee all national and state elections.
It operates through a structured hierarchy from the Chief Election Commissioner in Delhi down to District and Constituency officers (RO/ERO).
Key duties include setting election schedules, registering parties, updating voter lists, and proactively enforcing the Model Code of Conduct (MCC).
The Electoral Process and Scale: India's elections are a mammoth task, involving roughly 980 million voters and 1 million polling stations for 543 parliamentary constituencies.
The process is highly organized, involving four levels of polling officers for identity verification, inking, and recording votes on EVMs.
Reserved seats (84 for SC, 47 for ST) ensure the representation of marginalized communities in the Lok Sabha.
Technology and Inclusivity: Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) and VVPATs (paper trail) have replaced paper ballots to ensure transparency, security, and fast results.
The ECI has pioneered accessibility measures such as home voting for the elderly, braille voter cards, and ramps to ensure 'no voter is left behind'.
NOTA (None of the Above) provides a quiet but powerful tool for voters to register their dissatisfaction with all candidates.
Democratic Integrity and Challenges: The Model Code of Conduct (since 1960/1991) prevents the misuse of government machinery and ensures a level playing field for all candidates.
Modern challenges include the influence of money power, criminalization of politics, voter apathy in urban areas, and digital misinformation.
Democracy's endurance depends on an aware, vigilant, and responsible electorate participating in the 'Festival of Democracy'.
